patch.value: SDK lintExtensionBlueprint + assertExtensionBlueprint: author-time preflight for composeExtension blueprints (capability drift, duplicate commands, empty surfaces)

Add author-time preflight for declarative blueprints so the two footguns `composeExtension`'s own docstring names are caught at edit/test time instead of at activation:

1. `lintExtensionBlueprint(blueprint, options?): ExtensionBlueprintLintResult` (src/sdk/compose.ts) — pure data analysis, no activation. Structured findings:
   - `capability_undeclared` (ERROR): a capability the blueprint exercises is missing from the declared set; the loader throws `extension_capability_missing` at activation. The author-time form of that hard failure.
   - `capability_unused` (WARNING): a declared capability the blueprint never exercises; the runtime equivalent is the `pm package doctor` `extension_capability_unused` advisory (pm-fk84). This makes lintExtensionBlueprint the author-time inverse of `reconcileExtensionCapabilityUsage`, just as `deriveExtensionCapabilities` is for the used set.
   - `duplicate_command` (WARNING): the same normalized command path declared twice in `commands` (silent last-wins shadow at dispatch).
   - `command_override_conflict` (WARNING): a path declared as both a fresh `commands` definition and a `commandOverrides` override (contradictory).
   - `empty_surface` (WARNING): a registration field present but empty (e.g. `commands: []`) — a dead declaration.
   - `manifest_capabilities_absent` (WARNING): blueprint exercises capabilities but provides no declared set to reconcile against; suggests `capabilities: deriveExtensionCapabilities(blueprint)`.
   Declared set comes from `options.declaredCapabilities` or `blueprint.manifest.capabilities`, normalized through the same `normalizeKnownExtensionCapability` (legacy-alias aware) the runtime reconciler uses.

2. `assertExtensionBlueprint(blueprint, options?)` (src/sdk/testing.ts) — the assert* family member: runs the linter and throws (plain Error, matching the file convention) on any error-severity finding, returning the lint result on success so a test can still inspect warnings. One-liner CI guard for package authors.

Scope: SDK barrel + /sdk/testing re-export, docs/SDK.md (Declarative Authoring + Testing Helpers), 100% test + docstring coverage. Lives author-time/test-only (a blueprint is in-code data, not on disk), consistent with the assert*/run* family.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: lintExtensionBlueprint must resolve legacy capability aliases (migration/validation -> schema) on the author's declared set, even though the runtime reconcileExtensionCapabilityUsage only uses normalizeKnownExtensionCapability. Reason: the loader runs normalizeManifestCapabilities (which alias-resolves) BEFORE reconciliation, so by reconcile-time aliases are already canonical; but the author-time lint sees the RAW manifest.capabilities, so it must resolve aliases itself (resolveLegacyExtensionCapabilityAlias ?? normalizeKnownExtensionCapability) or it would emit a false-positive capability_undeclared error for a manifest that actually loads fine. Unknown capabilities are dropped (a separate diagnostic owns them) so a typo is not misreported as unused. capability_undeclared is the only ERROR severity (loader throws); everything else is advisory WARNING so ok stays true.

patch.value: Shipped lintExtensionBlueprint (structured author-time findings: capability_undeclared error + capability_unused/duplicate_command/command_override_conflict/empty_surface/manifest_capabilities_absent warnings) and assertExtensionBlueprint (throwing assert* family member). Legacy aliases resolved like the loader. Barrel + /sdk/testing exports, docs, 100% coverage, manual end-to-end verified.
Shipped lintExtensionBlueprint + assertExtensionBlueprint for author-time blueprint preflight.
Capability drift / duplicate / empty-surface footguns are caught at edit/test time, not at activation.
Both helpers added with structured findings, legacy-alias resolution, barrel + /sdk/testing exports, docs, 100% coverage.
